description:
Goal: fork the semantic native-codegen path into compiler2 ownership with behavior preserved. Signal: compiler2 has a compiler2-owned native backend entry path that can lower `NativeProgram(root)` end to end while still carrying any necessary `SpecPlan`/`SpecRegistry`-shaped baggage only as quarantined compiler2-local implementation detail.

Change:
- copy/adapt the native-codegen modules whose behavior depends on compiler2 facts out of `src/ir_codegen` into compiler2 with the smallest semantic delta necessary to preserve behavior
- keep truly universal substrate shared: Cranelift/module setup, symbol declaration/finalization, JIT/AOT packaging, and runtime ABI/layout mechanics
- wire compiler2-native tests to enter the compiler2-owned backend directly so parity is proven before front-door cutover

Why:
- compiler2 cannot cleanly simplify this backend while the semantic code still lives inside a shared path shaped around the old compiler

Checks:
- compiler2-native tests enter the compiler2-owned backend directly
- parity fixtures still pass through the forked path before front-door switch
- any copied planner-shaped baggage is confined to compiler2-owned modules rather than the shared route
title: Compiler2 native backends: fork semantic native codegen into compiler2
